pi_items.source: Task specification file (archisurance_specs.txt) generated by setup_task.sh states: 'Save source: ~/Diagrams/claims_architecture.drawio'
Task specification file (archisurance_specs.txt) generated by setup_task.sh states: 'Export PDF: ~/Diagrams/exports/claims_architecture.pdf'
Task specification file states in BUSINESS LAYER: 'Actor: "Customer"'
Task specification file states in BUSINESS LAYER: 'Service: "Submit Claim"'
Task specification file states in BUSINESS LAYER: 'Process: "Claims Administration"'
Task specification file states in APPLICATION LAYER: 'Service: "Claims Management Service"'
Task specification file states in APPLICATION LAYER: 'Component: "Home & Away Policy Administration"'
Task specification file states in APPLICATION LAYER: 'Data Object: "Customer Data"'
Task specification file states in TECHNOLOGY LAYER: 'Device: "Mainframe"'
Task specification file states in TECHNOLOGY LAYER: 'System Software: "Policy Database"'

Task specification defines 7 relationships: (1) Customer assigned to Submit Claim, (2) Submit Claim triggers Claims Administration, (3) Claims Management Service serves Claims Administration, (4) Home & Away realizes Claims Management Service, (5) Home & Away accesses Customer Data, (6) Mainframe hosts Home & Away, (7) Policy Database realizes Customer Data storage. Minimum of 5 is achievable.

privileged_info_summary: The task is entirely synthetic with no external data source. The task specification is procedurally generated by setup_task.sh at initialization time, creating an archisurance_specs.txt file that specifies requirements for modeling a Home & Away insurance claims system using ArchiMate in draw.io. The metadata claims enumerate the expected output filename (claims_architecture.drawio) and PDF export (claims_architecture.pdf), as well as 8 required diagram elements across three layers: Business (Customer, Submit Claim, Claims Administration), Application (Claims Management Service, Home & Away Policy Administration, Customer Data), and Technology (Mainframe, Policy Database). The specification defines 7 relationships between elements, satisfying the minimum connection requirement of 5. All metadata values are directly verified against the generated specification file.
